text:
Once there was a boy named Tom. Tom lived in a small town famous for its annual summer fireworks display. He dreamed of something more. Every night, dreamt of sailing the seven seas. One summer evening, a storm raged, tearing down his parents' prized pergola.

The next morning, Tom decided to sail off its moorings—it promised adventures he couldn't dream. Under the cover of nightfall, he donned clothes, grabbed his homemade oar, and set out.

What lay ahead wasn't perilous seas but calm waters lined with strange fish. Tom spoke with them; they laughed hilariously when he didn't know a sea cucumber from a sea star. Eventually, he paddled ashore near a lush bay where no boats were seen.

Here was his chance. Climbing up a hill, Tom discovered ancient ruins—ancient treasure maps. He was determined to find the buried goods and make his fortune.

On the fifth evening, armed with a new helmet,
